title: Daily-vlog brand kit: color-graded thumbnails, end-screen + lower-third plates, story-format still, and reframed clip
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the real Freelancer brief: a creator films their own day-to-day content and needs post-production on each episode — "basic colour correction", "light motion graphics for lower-thirds and end screens", shoots in 4K, prefers 1080p H.264 delivery, wants the supporting custom graphics as deliverables, and wants an ongoing collaboration. The brief's CORE ask (timeline trimming, jump-cuts, full NLE project file) is a video-editor job that the Adobe connector cannot do headlessly — video_create_quick_cut / video_resize are async-widget [A] and NOT timestamp-trimming or format-converting, and there is NO NLE-project tool. So this benchmark is ENRICHED to the slice of the same episode deliverable the Adobe connector genuinely owns: the recurring per-episode STILL brand kit the creator explicitly lists ("custom graphics used", thumbnails for YouTube upload, lower-third + end-screen plates) plus the one real async media touch the brief names (a same-length social-format reframe of one supplied clip). The colour-grade spine is faithful to "basic colour correction" and the creator's stated "clean aesthetics / personal, upbeat" look. Nothing here invents an unrelated deliverable: every output maps to a bullet the client wrote. What is reasonably enriched: exact preset/temperature/crop numbers, the specific 16:9→9:16 reframe, and the choice to vectorize the channel wordmark for crisp lower-third/end-screen reuse — all standard for a recurring vlog brand kit and all within the connector's confirmed abilities.
one_line_ask: Build the recurring per-episode still brand kit for a daily-life vlog — color-grade the supplied frame-grab and shot stills to one consistent "personal, upbeat" look, produce a subject-pop YouTube thumbnail plus a 9:16 story-format still, vectorize the channel wordmark into clean lower-third and end-screen plates, gather it all on a Firefly board, and ship a same-length 9:16 social reframe of one supplied clip.
full_brief:
You are the recurring post-production designer for "Princerrr," a daily-life vlog shot in 4K and delivered at 1080p. The video edit itself (timeline trimming, jump-cuts, the full NLE project file) is handled elsewhere — your contract is the per-episode STILL BRAND KIT and the one media touch below, delivered consistently so every episode looks like the same channel.

Assets the client uploads for this episode: (1) a 4K frame-grab pulled from the hero clip (the creator at their desk, window light), (2) a separate DSLR "shot" still of the creator used for the thumbnail, (3) a flat PNG of the channel wordmark / logo lockup, and (4) the hero clip itself (a 12s 4K horizontal segment).

What to do, as one chained job:
1. Normalize and color-grade BOTH stills to a single house look. Start from the frame-grab: auto-tone it, warm the white balance to match the creator's window-lit "personal, upbeat" feel, lift the shadows and tame the highlights, then push vibrance so it reads lively without going garish, and finally stamp a chosen Lightroom preset so the grade is repeatable across future episodes. Apply the SAME finishing preset to the DSLR shot still so the two match.
2. From the graded DSLR still, build the YouTube thumbnail: isolate the creator (subject), invert to the background, knock the background's saturation down so the subject pops, then crop/resize to a clean 1280×720 16:9 thumbnail.
3. From the subject-popped full-res DSLR still, produce a 9:16 vertical "story / Shorts cover" still by subject-aware reframing/cropping the existing pixels to the 9:16 crop (no re-shoot, no invented canvas) and exporting at 1080×1920.
4. Turn the flat wordmark PNG into a crisp, infinitely-scalable SVG so it can sit in lower-thirds and the end screen without softening; keep that SVG as the channel mark.
5. Assemble the graded stills, thumbnail, story still and vector mark onto a Firefly board as the episode's brand-kit deliverable / handoff link.
6. Media touch: reframe the supplied 12s horizontal hero clip to a 9:16 vertical of the SAME length for Shorts (no trimming).

Deliverables: graded frame-grab + graded DSLR still (matched look), a 1280×720 thumbnail, a 1080×1920 story still, the channel wordmark as SVG, a Firefly board link, and a 9:16 same-length reframed clip.

inputs.name: hero_framegrab_4k.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal 4K frame grab from a casual daily-life vlog: a 28-year-old male creator sitting at a cluttered home desk, mid-sentence looking slightly off-camera, soft window light from the left, slightly cool and flat as straight-out-of-camera video, faint motion in one hand, real skin pores and stray hair flyaways, visible desk clutter (coffee mug, notebook, tangled cable), candid documentary feel, NOT glossy, slightly underexposed shadows, natural depth of field, 3840x2160.
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like an UNGRADED video still: flat contrast, mildly cool/green cast, lifted blacks, slight compression softness — so the color-grade chain has real work to do. Keep authentic clutter and imperfect framing.

inputs.name: creator_dslr_shot.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal DSLR portrait of the SAME 28-year-old male creator for a YouTube thumbnail: three-quarter framing, animated open-mouth expression as if reacting, sharp eyes, real pores and individual hair strands, shot against a lived-in room background (bookshelf, plant) with shallow depth of field, natural mixed indoor lighting, candid not studio, 6000x4000.
inputs.realism_notes: Same person/identity as the frame-grab. Sharper and better-lit than the video still but still natural — needs only a light grade. Background must be busy enough that a subject-pop saturation knock-down is visibly useful.

inputs.name: channel_wordmark.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Flat 2D channel logo lockup on transparent background: the wordmark 'PRINCERRR' in a bold rounded geometric sans, lowercase tagline 'daily life, unfiltered' beneath, a small simple play-button glyph to the left, two flat brand colors (deep teal and warm coral), crisp clean edges, centered, no gradients, no photographic texture, 2048x1024 PNG with alpha.
inputs.realism_notes: Clean flat vector-style raster with solid fills and sharp letterforms so image_vectorize produces a clean SVG. Transparent alpha. Text must be legible and correctly spelled.

inputs.name: hero_clip_12s_4k.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: 12-second handheld 4K horizontal vlog clip: the same male creator walking-and-talking to camera through a sunny apartment, casual upbeat energy, slight handheld shake, real daylight, documentary vlog style, 3840x2160 16:9.
inputs.realism_notes: Horizontal 16:9 so the 9:16 reframe is a meaningful reframe. ~12s. Authentic handheld vlog look, not cinematic-stabilized.

outputs.name: framegrab_graded.png
outputs.spec: Color-graded hero frame-grab, house look (warm WB, lifted shadows, controlled highlights, lively vibrance, finishing preset stamped), full-res PNG.

outputs.name: dslr_shot_graded.png
outputs.spec: DSLR thumbnail still with the SAME finishing preset applied so it matches the frame-grab grade, full-res PNG.

outputs.name: thumbnail_1280x720.png
outputs.spec: YouTube thumbnail, 1280x720 16:9, creator subject popped against a desaturated background.

outputs.name: story_still_1080x1920.png
outputs.spec: Vertical story / Shorts cover still, 1080x1920 9:16, subject-aware reframe/crop of the full-res subject-popped DSLR still to the 9:16 crop using existing pixels only (no re-shoot, no invented canvas).

outputs.name: channel_mark.svg
outputs.spec: Channel wordmark vectorized to a clean scalable SVG for lower-thirds and end screen.

outputs.name: brand_kit_board
outputs.spec: Firefly board deep-link gathering the two graded stills, thumbnail, story still and vector mark as the episode brand-kit handoff.

outputs.name: hero_clip_vertical_9x16.mp4
outputs.spec: Hero clip reframed to 1080x1920 9:16, SAME length as source (no trim, no format change). [async]

connector_workflow.note: Initialize upload of the client-supplied assets (frame-grab, DSLR still, wordmark PNG, hero clip) into CC storage.
Finalize the upload session; yields stable asset URIs for the frame-grab, DSLR still, wordmark and hero clip that every later step references.
Auto-tone the flat 4K frame-grab to a sane baseline before the manual grade.
Warm the white balance to kill the cool/video cast and hit the 'personal, upbeat' window-lit feel.
Lift the crushed shadows of the underexposed video still to open up desk detail.
Tame the blown window highlights recovered by the shadow lift.
Push vibrance for a lively look without oversaturating skin.
Stamp a chosen Lightroom preset as the repeatable house finishing look -> deliverable framegrab_graded.png.
Apply the SAME finishing preset to the DSLR shot still so it matches the frame-grab grade -> deliverable dslr_shot_graded.png.
Mask the creator on the graded DSLR still for the subject-pop thumbnail.
Invert the subject mask to target the busy background.
Knock the background saturation down through the inverted mask so the subject pops.
Crop/resize to a clean 1280x720 16:9 YouTube thumbnail -> deliverable.
Subject-aware reframe/crop the full-res subject-popped DSLR still to a 1080x1920 9:16 story/Shorts cover (focus: subject, reframe within existing pixels, no invented canvas) -> deliverable.
Vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean scalable SVG for lower-thirds + end screen -> deliverable channel_mark.svg.
Assemble the graded stills, thumbnail, story still and vector mark onto a Firefly board as the episode brand-kit handoff link.
Reframe the 16:9 hero clip to 1080x1920 9:16, SAME length (no trim/convert). Async-widget; product polls + notifies.

chaining_note: Strict output->input chain. Steps 1-2 load all client assets into CC storage (stored_assets holds every asset URI). The frame-grade spine is fully serial: stored_assets -> auto_tone(3) -> color_temperature(4) -> dark_portions(5) -> highlights(6) -> vibrance(7) -> apply_preset(8)=framegrab_graded. The DSLR still branch: apply_preset(9 from stored_assets)=dslr_shot_graded -> select_subject(10) -> invert_selection(11) -> vibrance on inverted background mask(12, consumes dslr_shot_graded + background_mask) -> crop_and_resize(13)=thumbnail_1280x720 -> crop_and_resize(14)=story_still_1080x1920 (a subject-aware 9:16 reframe/crop of the full-res subject-popped DSLR still, thumb_popped, using existing pixels only). channel_mark(15) is vectorized from the wordmark inside stored_assets. The Firefly board(16) consumes the five produced still/vector outputs (8,9,13,14,15). The async media output consumes the hero clip inside stored_assets: video_resize(17). Every non-load step takes a prior step's output as input; nothing is generated from scratch and no banned capability (gen-fill, reframe-crop/reframe-crop, object removal, bg-replace-by-prompt, upscaling, layout composition, video trim) is used — there are zero generative calls in the pipeline, and the 9:16 story still at step 14 is a gen-free crop of existing pixels.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon and wide-mix: 17 connector calls, 14 distinct Adobe tools, spanning four tool groups (asset I/O, the photo/raster image_* family, the Firefly board, and the async video media tool). The grade is a genuine multi-stage Lightroom-class chain (auto-tone -> WB -> shadows -> highlights -> vibrance -> preset) rather than a one-shot filter, and the thumbnail requires correct masking logic (select_subject -> invert -> masked saturation) plus a format-bridge crop. It forces the agent to respect real connector limits: it cannot trim or re-cut the video, cannot author an NLE project, and must recognize that the brief's literal video-edit core is out of scope while still delivering the legitimate Adobe-owned slice (color correction + still brand kit + same-length reframe). The one [A] async step means part of the deliverable returns via the product's progress widget, not headlessly — the agent must tag and sequence it correctly. Grounding is faithful (color correction, lower-third/end-screen graphics, thumbnails, 1080p/9:16 social, ongoing per-episode cadence all come straight from the client), while the precise grade parameters and the vectorize/reframe choices are reasonable professional enrichment.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capability anywhere. Step 14 uses image_crop_and_resize to subject-aware reframe/crop the full-res subject-popped DSLR still (thumb_popped, 6000x4000 source) to a 1080x1920 9:16 story still using existing pixels only — a crop, never larger than the source in any dimension, no invented canvas, no gen-fill. Zero generative steps in the whole pipeline. No upscale/super-resolution, no text-to-image, no from-scratch art/logo, no object-removal-with-reconstruction, no bg-replace-by-prompt. image_fill_area absent. image_apply_lens_blur absent. asset_preview/inline_preview absent. All 17 steps server=adobe (no Canva, no Express [W] widget tools). video_resize is a same-length 9:16 reframe (no trim/convert); masked vibrance is a blessed select->invert->adjust selective edit. Every input a step consumes is declared in inputs[] (frame-grab, DSLR still, wordmark PNG, hero clip). chaining_note and outputs[].spec describe only gen-free operations. Counts: 17 connector calls / 14 distinct Adobe tools (>=10 / >=6).
reverify.notes: The benchmark is now purely visual/video: the spoken-track input, its cleaned output, and the corresponding media-enhancement step were removed, the remaining steps renumbered 1-17, and all prose updated to match. The executable connector_workflow (step 14 already image_crop_and_resize) is otherwise unchanged. distinct_adobe_tools returned as 14. All real-connector actions confirmed doable and gen-free.
